episodes.overview: Jimmy has a new endeavor. Chuck pushes himself to the limit. Nacho finds himself in a power struggle.

episodes.name: Expenses
episodes.overview: Jimmy tries to settle debts. Nacho reunites with an acquaintance. Mike helps Stacey with a project and makes a connection.

episodes.name: Slip
episodes.overview: Jimmy is pushed to desperate measures. Nacho picks up a new skill. Mike explores an alliance. Kim stands up to Hamlin.

episodes.name: Fall
episodes.overview: Jimmy visits an old friend and takes up a new pastime. Chuck and Hamlin spar over the future of the firm. Kim faces challenges at work.

episodes.name: Lantern
episodes.overview: Kim takes time off. Jimmy tries to make amends. Nacho gambles with his future. Hamlin pushes Chuck to make a decision.

episodes.name: Smoke
episodes.overview: Jimmy struggles to cope with Chuck's tragic death. Mike ponders his role at Madrigal. Howard makes a startling confession.

episodes.name: Breathe
episodes.overview: Jimmy seeks new employment. Gus navigates the fallout from Hector's collapse. Kim endeavors to support Jimmy in the wake of Chuck's death.

episodes.name: Something Beautiful
episodes.overview: Jimmy puts a risky plan into motion. As alliances shift, Nacho finds himself in the crosshairs. Kim contemplates her future.

episodes.name: Talk
episodes.overview: A restless Jimmy embarks on a new endeavor while Mike burns bridges. Kim pursues her bliss. Nacho tries to survive a turf war.

episodes.name: Quite a Ride
episodes.overview: Jimmy identifies a new market for his talents. Mike vets a potential partnership. Kim drives a hard bargain.

episodes.name: Piñata
episodes.overview: While Jimmy dreams of Wexler-McGill, Kim makes a bold move. Mike puts a plan into motion for Gus.

episodes.name: Something Stupid
episodes.overview: Jimmy expands his business, but runs into a problem that only Kim can solve. Gus intervenes in Hector's medical care. Mike deals with a setback.

episodes.name: Coushatta
episodes.overview: Jimmy goes to great lengths to right a wrong, as Kim pulls out all the stops for a case. Mike lets his team blow off steam. Nacho receives a visitor.

episodes.name: Wiedersehen
episodes.overview: Jimmy and Kim unburden themselves, risking their relationship in the process. Nacho is forced to make the rounds with Lalo. Mike has cause to worry.

episodes.name: Winner
episodes.overview: Jimmy turns the page on his reputation. Lalo tracks a loose end in Gus' operation. Mike is forced to make a difficult decision.

episodes.name: Magic Man
episodes.overview: Now doing business as Saul Goodman, Jimmy unveils an unorthodox strategy for client development that tests Kim's tolerance of his new legal persona. Lalo searches for the mysterious Michael, only to discover a problem within his own operation.

episodes.name: 50% Off
episodes.overview: Jimmy's promotional stunt has unintended consequences. Nacho takes extreme measures to earn Lalo's trust. Saul is a success at the courthouse but still has yet to win Kim over. Simmering with rage and self-loathing, Mike loses his temper.

episodes.name: The Guy for This
episodes.overview: Jimmy's business enters uncharted territory. Kim's confidence is tested when she's faced with a legal problem only she can solve. Nacho navigates increased pressure from Gus. Mike continues to spiral.

episodes.name: Namaste
episodes.overview: As Jimmy doubles down on Saul Goodman, a deeply conflicted Kim brings him an interesting proposition, Gus makes a sacrifice in order to play the long game, and Mike attempts to smooth things over with his family.

episodes.name: Dedicado a Max
episodes.overview: Mike takes an impromptu trip out of town. As she and Jimmy grow closer over their campaign to help a client, Kim makes a surprising call.

episodes.name: Wexler v. Goodman
episodes.overview: Kim tries to pump the brakes on Jimmy's latest scheme, but it may be too late to stop Saul. Mike turns the heat up on Lalo. Nacho attempts to enlist support from above.

episodes.name: JMM
episodes.overview: Jimmy and Kim build a legal firewall with help from Huell. Kim sets things straight with her clients as Jimmy is compromised by his. Gus, at war on all fronts, calms troubled waters in service of his plot to build an empire. Mike does damage control.

episodes.name: Bagman
episodes.overview: When a simple errand for a client goes sideways, Jimmy is pushed to the limit. Mike takes measures to contain the wrath of the cartel. Lalo gets an unexpected visitor.

episodes.name: Bad Choice Road
episodes.overview: In the wake of Jimmy's traumatic misadventure, Kim takes stock of what is important. Jimmy attempts to return to business as usual, but it's more difficult than anticipated. Gus and Mike set a plan in motion.

episodes.name: Something Unforgivable
episodes.overview: Jimmy and Kim make a sideways move that takes a serious turn. Nacho gets closer to the cartel than he'd like.

episodes.name: Wine and Roses
episodes.overview: Nacho runs for his life. Jimmy and Kim hatch a plan. Mike questions his allegiances.

episodes.name: Carrot and Stick
episodes.overview: Harsh realities dawn on Nacho. Gus investigates his suspicions.

episodes.name: Rock and Hard Place
episodes.overview: Still on the run, Nacho is forced to choose where his loyalties lie. Jimmy doubles down.

episodes.name: Hit and Run
episodes.overview: Gus takes extreme measures. Jimmy and Kim enlist a local pro to put on a show.

episodes.name: Black and Blue
episodes.overview: While business booms for Jimmy, the vise tightens on the cat-and-mouse game between Gus and Lalo.

episodes.name: Axe and Grind
episodes.overview: Kim and Jimmy enlist a knowledgeable contact. Howard scrutinizes Jimmy's business practices.

episodes.name: Plan and Execution
episodes.overview: Jimmy and Kim deal with a last-minute snag. Howard's investigation finally yields results.

episodes.name: Point and Shoot
episodes.overview: An unexpected visitor forces Jimmy and Kim to face the consequences of their actions.

episodes.name: Fun and Games
episodes.overview: Gus attempts to smooth things over with the cartel while Mike ties up loose ends.

episodes.name: Nippy
episodes.overview: A lost dog leads to a new friend. A new player gets in the game.

episodes.name: Breaking Bad
episodes.overview: Emboldened by recent successes, partners throw caution to the wind and escalate their enterprise to new levels.

episodes.name: Waterworks
episodes.overview: The stakes are raised when a discovery is made that will send shockwaves far and wide.

episodes.name: Saul Gone
episodes.overview: Revelations. Regrets. RICO violations. Jimmy's worlds — and identities — converge in one final showdown.

episodes.name: Ejaculation
episodes.overview: As Andrew falls under the spell of the randy Hormone Monster, his buddy Nick becomes obsessed with the lack of changes in his own body.

episodes.name: Everybody Bleeds
episodes.overview: A field trip goes from awkward to mortifying as Jessi navigates the demands of coupledom and gets her first period -- inside the Statue of Liberty.

episodes.name: Am I Gay?
episodes.overview: A trailer for a new movie starring The Rock leaves Andrew questioning his sexuality. Jessi and Nick's budding romance turns ugly fast.

episodes.name: Sleepover: A Harrowing Ordeal of Emotional Brutality
episodes.overview: While the boys head to Jay's for a testosterone-fueled sleepover, Jessi throws a party of her own and gets caught up in mean-girl drama.

episodes.name: Girls Are Horny Too
episodes.overview: A steamy novel making the rounds at school inspires Jessi to explore her own sexuality -- and leaves the boys totally confused about what girls want.

episodes.name: Pillow Talk
episodes.overview: Nick drags Andrew into the city to visit an old camp crush, but the day doesn't go as planned. Jay embarks on an emotional journey with his pillow.

episodes.name: Requiem for a Wet Dream
episodes.overview: Andrew struggles to keep the Hormone Monster in check as he works with Missy on a science fair project. Jessi and Matthew have fun at Jay's expense.

episodes.name: The Head Push
episodes.overview: When Leah hosts a party for the high school drama crowd, Nick wrestles with a romantic dilemma, and Andrew and Missy witness a disturbing scene.

episodes.name: I Survived Jessi's Bat Mitzvah
episodes.overview: Family blowups, secret liaisons and an unflattering dress make Jessi's bat mitzvah a night to remember.

episodes.name: The Pornscape
episodes.overview: Andrew tries to drown his sorrows in a sea of porn, only to become hopelessly addicted. Coach Steve becomes a suspect in a murder spree.

episodes.name: Am I Normal?
episodes.overview: Andrew struggles to blend in after a growth spurt, Nick worries his hormones are out of whack, and Jessi and Jay get a taste of the teen runaway life.

episodes.name: What Is It About Boobs?
episodes.overview: A classmate's booming bosom sends the boys into a rapture -- and leaves the girls with a raging case of insecurity. Coach Steve drops a bombshell.

episodes.name: The Shame Wizard
episodes.overview: As Nick tries to figure out whether he has a shot with Gina, a mortifying encounter with Leah sends Andrew into a spiral of shame.

episodes.name: Steve the Virgin
episodes.overview: Coach Steve finally gets his chance to do sex on a lady, Nick and Andrew go on a double date, and Jessi's rebellious streak leads to big trouble.

episodes.name: The Planned Parenthood Show
episodes.overview: An argument in sex ed class turns into a skit-tastic journey through the worlds of Planned Parenthood, contraceptives and STDs.

episodes.name: Drug Buddies
episodes.overview: Jessi and Nick dip into her dad's stash of edibles, with mind-bending results. Andrew searches for the right moment to break up with Lola.

episodes.name: Guy Town
episodes.overview: While helping Greg move into a seedy bachelor complex, the boys and their dads debate what women want -- and what it means to be a man.

episodes.name: Dark Side of the Boob
episodes.overview: A school sleepover turns messy as Nick dishes on a makeout session and the Shame Wizard works his miserable magic on the whole class.

episodes.name: Smooch or Share
episodes.overview: Nick tries to lighten the mood at the sleepover with a new twist on Truth or Dare. The Shame Wizard meets his match. Jessi vows to change her ways.

episodes.name: The Department of Puberty
episodes.overview: After traveling through a magic portal to puberty headquarters, Nick seeks out a new Hormone Monster and Jessi bonds with the Depression Kitty.

episodes.name: My Furry Valentine
episodes.overview: Sappy cards. Stupid clichés. And so much pressure. For Andrew, Nick, Jessi and friends, Valentine's Day is a total freaking minefield.

episodes.name: Girls Are Angry Too
episodes.overview: When a shop class mishap inspires a sexist dress code, Jessi rebels, Missy struggles to keep her alter ego in check, and Andrew gets a wake-up call.

episodes.name: Cellsea
episodes.overview: Nick is bewitched by his cool new phone. A call from "Pharmacy Boy" turns Matthew into a nervous wreck. Jay finds a Netflix show made just for him.

episodes.name: Obsessed
episodes.overview: Cellsea goads Nick into posting a risqué video, Maury urges Matthew to speed things up with Aiden, and Jay feeds Missy ideas for her erotic fiction.

episodes.name: Florida
episodes.overview: While Nick tags along with the Gloubermans on a wild road trip to Florida, Jay discovers he's been "Home Alone'd" -- and moves in with a new family.

episodes.name: How To Have An Orgasm
episodes.overview: Connie gives Jessi a crash course in female pleasure, Maury tries to talk Andrew into sending a dick pic, and Nick lashes out at his new housemate.

episodes.name: Duke
episodes.overview: The ghost of Duke Ellington takes Nick and friends on a journey back in time to 1913: the year he lost his virginity and found his true calling.

episodes.name: Rankings
episodes.overview: As the friends feverishly swap lists of the hottest kids at school, newcomer Ali stokes the flames by announcing she's pansexual.

episodes.name: The ASSes
episodes.overview: A big standardized test sends everyone into a panic -- except for Jay, who just scored an Adderall prescription.

episodes.name: Disclosure the Movie: The Musical!
episodes.overview: A provocative school musical unleashes Missy's inner thespian -- and her new Hormone Monstress. The "Queer Eye" Fab Five pay a visit to Coach Steve.

episodes.name: Super Mouth
episodes.overview: When a freak storm gives everyone new superpowers, tensions between Andrew, Nick and friends boil over into an epic showdown.

episodes.name: The New Me
episodes.overview: At sleep-away camp, Jessi befriends her trans cabinmate, and Nick discovers his two best friends have a little too much in common.

episodes.name: The Hugest Period Ever
episodes.overview: While Jessi deals with menstrual mayhem, Nick tries to avoid showering with the guys, and Missy gets a makeover from her cousins in Atlanta.

episodes.name: Poop Madness
episodes.overview: On the last night of camp, bowel problems, a talent show and a hookup lead to hurt feelings. Back home, Jay and Lola throw a pool party.

episodes.name: Cafeteria Girls
episodes.overview: After seeing their eighth grade classmates coupled up, Nick and Andrew make a play for two seventh grade girls. Jessi adjusts to life in the city.

episodes.name: A Very Special 9/11 Episode
episodes.overview: On a trip to the 9/11 museum, Nick and Andrew sneak off to meet Jessi, Devon teaches Missy about code-switching, and Coach Steve gets a rude surprise.

episodes.name: Nick Starr
episodes.overview: Thirty years in the future, rich but lonely game show host Nick prepares to flee a dying Earth. But first, he has to track down the perfect plus-one.

episodes.name: Four Stories About Hand Stuff
episodes.overview: The kids at Bridgeton Middle explore enthusiastic consent, feminine pleasure, coercion and more in four short films.

episodes.name: The Funeral
episodes.overview: Wracked with guilt, Andrew tries to keep his urges in check. Matthew's mom ices him out of the church bake=off after reading his texts.

episodes.name: Horrority House
episodes.overview: A trippy haunted house hosted by Kappa Kappa Kill forces each of the friends to reckon with their biggest fears.

episodes.name: What Are You Gonna Do?
episodes.overview: Forced out of his own body by his smug alter ego, Nick fights back - with help from his friends and Gratitoad. Matthew comes out to his dad.

episodes.name: No Nut November

episodes.name: The Shane Lizard Rises
episodes.overview: Eager to get his mojo back, the Shame Wizard drops in on swim class to torment the kids with bodily insecurities.

episodes.name: Lovebugs

episodes.name: The Green-Eyed Monster
episodes.overview: Unrequited love and jealous rivalries turn the friends into seething beasts. Andrew gets a little too invested in a substitute teacher.

episodes.name: Thanksgiving
episodes.overview: Missy tries to show her cousins she's not a dork, Andrew takes a stand against his dad's turkey tyranny, and Jesse hopes for a Thanksgiving miracle.

episodes.name: Best Friends Make the Best Lovers
episodes.overview: While Jessi sorts through her feelings for Ali, Nick joins Devon for a boys' night, Andrew shows off his new persona, and Missy starts a rumor online.

episodes.name: I F**king Hate You

episodes.name: Re-New Year's Eve
episodes.overview: Nick follows Walter to the land of monsters and demands to see the person in charge. A New Year's Eve party inspires a flurry of grand gestures.

episodes.name: Twenty Two and You
episodes.overview: A DNA test leads Nick to uncover a wee family secret. Jay insists on introducing Matthew to the Bilzerian clan. Andrew puts his lovebug to the test.

episodes.name: Vagina Shame
episodes.overview: From itching to grooming to late blooming: There's no shortage of fodder for shame maven Rita St. Swithens in this special episode.

episodes.name: Rice Purity Test
episodes.overview: A viral quiz about naughty deeds sparks arguments and unlikely new friendships. Matthew plans a baby shower for Maury.

episodes.name: Andrew's Gonna Touch A Boob Tonight
episodes.overview: While Andrew prepares for a momentous evening with Bernie, Nick sets out to meet his secret grandfather, and Jay cooks up some romance for Matthew.

episodes.name: The Apple Brooch
episodes.overview: The voice of a slick, new tech accessory hypnotizes people into doing things they wouldn't normally do. Missy grapples with the concept of sin.

episodes.name: Dadda Dia!
episodes.overview: Lola hits the jackpot — in more ways than one. Missy takes her dad to Elijah's church. The Birches make a plan to get rid of their new housemate.

episodes.name: Asexual Healing
episodes.overview: Elijah tries to figure out why he doesn't have the same urges as his friends. A jealous Andrew hops a train to Vermont to surprise Bernie

episodes.name: The Parents Aren't Alright
episodes.overview: While Andrew and Nick deal with parental crises, Jessi meets her new sibling, and Matthew realizes he's made Jay into a different person.

episodes.name: F**ked Up Friday
episodes.overview: A sudden outbreak of body-swapping yields awkward situations and poignant revelations galore.

episodes.name: Big Mouth's Going to High School (But Not for Nine More Episodes)
episodes.overview: With two months left in eighth grade, the incoming freshmen tour their new high school with junior hosts while Jay evades his terrifying brothers.

episodes.name: Epididymitis
episodes.overview: A doctor puts Andrew on testicular rest, Nick asks Jay to help him get in shape for high school and Jessi becomes fascinated by Caitlin's breastfeeding.

episodes.name: The Ambition Gremlin
episodes.overview: With no distractions, Andrew's schoolwork improves — winning his father's praise. Jessi goes a little too far when trying to emulate cool, mature Leah.

episodes.name: Day Tripping
episodes.overview: While Andrew and Nick follow Judd to Brooklyn to score drugs, Jay gets jealous of Kurt and Lola's relationship, and Jessi oversteps at Matthew's house.

episodes.name: Graduation
episodes.overview: As graduation day nears, Nick must decide if he will attend Cobblestones Academy while Andrew goes on an apology tour after being voted "Grossest Human."

episodes.name: The International Show
episodes.overview: Big Mouth goes global for a very special episode as Maury and Connie take a closer look at how kids around the world experience puberty.

episodes.name: Get the F**k Outta My House
episodes.overview: Andrew takes a liking to his new neighbor — and her mom. Nick wants to be more than Danni's friend, but her entanglement with Travis gets in the way.

episodes.name: The Bad Hookup
episodes.overview: At their first high school party, Andrew discovers his talent for drinking games, Nick hopes for a shot with Danni, and Missy meets a potential rebound.

episodes.name: Panic! At the Mall
episodes.overview: During a sleepover, Jay teaches Andrew how to defend himself against bullies. Then, Missy gets overwhelmed while back-to-school shopping with Jessi.

episodes.name: A Finger in Time
episodes.overview: The Big Mouth kids start high school! Andrew looks inside himself to solve his problem with Pumbaa while dread stops Missy from attending her first day.

episodes.name: Homecumming
episodes.overview: With a big dance approaching, FOMO's everywhere: Missy misses school, Matthew dives into the deep end — and Andrew suspects that Other Andrew is evil.

episodes.name: Cliques, Pricks, and Sonic's Dick
episodes.overview: Jessi and Jay pair up to parent a melon "baby," Missy faces her fears, and Nick and Other Andrew try to fit in with a group of ultra-rich kids.

episodes.name: Why Do We Go Through Puberty?
episodes.overview: After Nick has a sudden growth spurt, Ms. Dunn takes the class inside the body of a teenager to learn about the wonders of puberty.

episodes.name: Lola Skumpy: License to Drive
episodes.overview: A reckless Lola gets behind the wheel, and Jessi and her mom have a really big fight. Nick's mother finds his "special shirt" while cleaning his room.

episodes.name: Am I Smoking Too Much Weed?
episodes.overview: Depression Kitty convinces an anxious Jessi to care less. Nick attracts Devin's attention, and the Robotics Club takes an overnight field trip.

episodes.name: Everyone Watches Porn
episodes.overview: Matthew explores his sexual preferences, Nick says the wrong thing, and Missy uses Andrew to make Nate jealous. Jessi finds a surprise on Camden's phone.

episodes.name: Have Some Goddamn Compassion
episodes.overview: Connie recruits a Compassion Pachyderm to teach the kids how to see things from another point of view. Andrew sets out to steal Missy's man.

episodes.name: Horny Talky Yes Please
episodes.overview: Missy's Lovebug steps in to solve a problem, and Jessi practices enthusiastic consent. Nick tries life without porn. Lola gives Jay's dog a makeover.

episodes.name: Everything We Forgot to Tell You About Sex
episodes.overview: In honor of the show's next-to-last episode, Maury and Connie dive into the "Big Mouth" mailbag to answer curious fans' burning questions.

episodes.name: The Great Unknown
episodes.overview: As the kids creep closer to adulthood, a memorable finale takes them back to where it all began to say goodbye — and face the future.

episodes.name: The National Anthem
episodes.overview: Prime Minister Michael Callow faces a shocking dilemma when Princess Susannah, a much-loved member of the Royal Family, is kidnapped.

episodes.name: Fifteen Million Merits
episodes.overview: After failing to impress the judges on a singing competition show, a woman must either perform degrading acts or return to a slave-like existence.

episodes.name: The Entire History of You

episodes.name: Be Right Back

episodes.overview: Victoria wakes up and cannot remember anything about her life. Everyone she encounters refuses to communicate with her.

episodes.overview: After a virus infects his laptop, a teen faces a daunting choice: carry out orders delivered by text message, or risk having intimate secrets exposed.

episodes.name: Men Against Fire

episodes.name: Arkangel
episodes.overview: Worried about her daughter's safety, single mom Marie signs up for a cutting-edge device that monitors the girl's whereabouts -- and much more.

episodes.overview: Architect Mia scrambles to keep a dark secret under wraps, while insurance investigator Shazia harvests people's memories of a nearby accident scene.

episodes.name: Metalhead
episodes.overview: At an abandoned warehouse, scavengers searching for supplies encounter a ruthless foe and flee for their lives through a bleak wasteland.

episodes.name: Striking Vipers
episodes.overview: When old college friends Danny and Karl reconnect in a VR version of their favorite video game, the late-night sessions yield an unexpected discovery.

episodes.name: Smithereens

episodes.name: Rachel, Jack and Ashley Too

episodes.overview: An average woman discovers a global streaming service has adapted her daily life — and her secrets — into a drama starring A-list actress Salma Hayek.

episodes.name: Loch Henry
episodes.overview: While filming a nature documentary in a sleepy Scottish town, a young couple catches wind of a juicy local story with ties to shocking past events.

episodes.name: Mazey Day

episodes.name: Demon 79
episodes.overview: Northern England, 1979. A meek sales assistant discovers she must commit terrible acts to prevent an imminent disaster.

episodes.name: Common People
episodes.overview: When a teacher is left fighting for her life, her doting husband signs her up for a high-tech system that keeps her alive — but at a cost.

episodes.overview: A young woman is unnerved when an old acquaintance joins the company she works at — especially as there's something odd about her that only she can see.

episodes.overview: Nanette Cole and the crew of the USS Callister are stranded in an infinite virtual universe, fighting for survival against 30 million players.

episodes.name: Pilot

episodes.overview: Walter fights with Jesse over his drug use, causing him to leave Walter alone with their captive, Krazy-8. Meanwhile, Hank has a scared straight moment with Walter Jr. after his aunt discovers he has been smoking pot. Also, Skylar is upset when Walter stays away from home.

episodes.name: Cancer Man
episodes.overview: Walter finally tells his family that he has been stricken with cancer. Meanwhile, the DEA believes Albuquerque has a new, big time player to worry about. Meanwhile, a worthy recipient is the target of a depressed Walter's anger, and Jesse makes a surprise visit to his parents home.

episodes.overview: Walt faces a new threat, on a new front and deals with an increasingly angry Skyler, who must consider what to do next with her life and the kids. Meanwhile, Jesse comes face-to-face with some startling self-revelations.

episodes.overview: As Hank's hospital bills stack up, Skyler hatches a plan. Walt and Gus come to a better understanding. Jesse, looking for more independence, pursues a new opportunity.

episodes.overview: Skyler gets involved with Walt's business. Hank struggles with his recovery. Jesse makes a startling discovery after meeting a girl at group therapy.

episodes.overview: With Jesse on the run and Mike in hot pursuit, Walt negotiates a bargain with Gus and concocts a disturbing plan to provide for his and Jesse's safety.

episodes.overview: Walt and Jesse face the deadly consequences of their actions. Skyler deals with a puzzling disappearance, as Marie struggles to help Hank with his recovery.

episodes.overview: Walt attempts to form a new alliance as he plans his next move. Skyler pushes Walt towards a business opportunity, in hopes of protecting the family.

episodes.overview: Skyler makes an unsettling discovery. Walter, Jr. pushes his dad into a questionable purchase. Jesse offers Mike some unexpected help.

episodes.overview: Skyler's past mistakes come back to haunt her. Gus takes action to thwart his rivals. Jesse seeks Walt's help, with mixed results.

episodes.overview: Walt takes drastic action to protect his secret and Gus. Skyler's efforts to solve Ted's financial problems hit a wall.

episodes.overview: Hank pushes Gomez to pursue one last lead, while Walt struggles to protect the family. Jesse gets alarming news and rushes to meet with Mr. White.

episodes.overview: Walt and Jesse team up to take on Gus. With Saul's help, Walt finds an unexpected ally.

episodes.overview: As Walt deals with the aftermath of the Casa Tranquila explosion, Hank works to wrap up his investigation of Gus' empire.

episodes.overview: Walt and Jesse pursue an unlikely business partner. The DEA filters through various leads in hopes of finding something.

episodes.overview: Walt and Jesse formulate a new business plan. Walt shares a secret with Marie.

episodes.name: Fifty-One
episodes.overview: Walt celebrates another birthday. Skyler contemplates her options, and an associate puts a crimp in Walt and Jesse's business strategy.

episodes.name: Dead Freight
episodes.overview: Walt's team gets creative to obtain the methylamine they need to continue their operation.

episodes.name: Buyout
episodes.overview: Walt, Jesse, and Mike struggle over the future of their business, as occupational hazards weigh on Jesse.

episodes.name: Say My Name
episodes.overview: Walt takes control of business matters; Mike deals with the consequences of his actions.

episodes.name: Gliding Over All
episodes.overview: Walt takes care of loose ends; Walt makes a dangerous decision.

episodes.name: Blood Money
episodes.overview: As Walt and Jesse adjust to life out of the business, Hank grapples with a troubling lead.

episodes.name: Buried
episodes.overview: While Skyler's past catches up with her, Walt covers his tracks. Jesse continues to struggle with his guilt.

episodes.overview: Jesse decides to make a change, while Walt and Skyler try to deal with an unexpected demand.

episodes.name: Rabid Dog
episodes.overview: An unusual strategy starts to bear fruit, while plans are set in motion that could change everything.

episodes.name: To'hajiilee
episodes.overview: Things heat up for Walt in unexpected ways.

episodes.name: Ozymandias
episodes.overview: Everyone copes with radically changed circumstances.

episodes.name: Granite State
episodes.overview: Events set in motion long ago move toward a conclusion.

episodes.name: Felina
episodes.overview: All bad things must come to an end.
April 26, 1986, Ukrainian SSR. Plant workers and firefighters put their lives on the line to control a catastrophic 1986 explosion at a Soviet nuclear power plant.

episodes.name: Please Remain Calm
episodes.overview: With untold millions at risk after the Chernobyl explosion, nuclear physicist Ulana Khomyuk makes a desperate attempt to reach Valery Legasov, a leading Soviet nuclear physicist, and warn him about the threat of second explosion that could devastate the continent.

episodes.name: Open Wide, O Earth
episodes.overview: Lyudmilla Ignatenko, a Pripyat resident, ignores warning about her firefighter husband's contamination. Valery Legasov lays out a decontamination plan, complete with human risks.

episodes.name: The Happiness of All Mankind
episodes.overview: Valery Legasov and Soviet Deputy Prime Minister Boris Shcherbina consider using lunar rovers to remove radioactive debris, while Ulana Khomyuk faces government hurdles in determining the truth about the cause of the explosion.

episodes.name: Vichnaya Pamyat
episodes.overview: Valery Legasov, Boris Shcherbina and Ulana Khomyuk risk their lives and reputations to expose the truth about Chernobyl.

episodes.name: Dexter
episodes.overview: Dexter's world is rocked when a rival serial murderer, dubbed the Ice Truck Killer by the media, privately contacts him and reveals that he knows Dexter's grisly secret. Meanwhile, Dexter's sister, Debra, is transferred to Homicide.

episodes.name: Crocodile
episodes.overview: The Miami Metro Police Department investigate the murder of an undercover officer, while Dexter stalks another victim. Debra discovers a piece of evidence in the Ice Truck Killer Case that helps her get a promotion.

episodes.name: Popping Cherry
episodes.overview: While LaGuerta focuses the department's attention on a potential suspect in the Ice Truck Killer Case, Debra continues to look elsewhere. Doakes pays a visit to drug lord, Carlos Guerrero. Flashbacks show Dexter killing his first human.

episodes.name: Let's Give the Boy a Hand
episodes.overview: The Ice Truck Killer's recent crime scenes send a secret message to Dexter Morgan, and lead him to remember his past.

episodes.name: Love American Style
episodes.overview: The Homicide Division is buzzing after The Ice Truck Killer's latest victim is found mutilated, while Dexter stalks a murderous human trafficker.

episodes.name: Return to Sender
episodes.overview: The Ice Truck Killer leaves a clue at the scene where Dexter has dispatched his latest victim, and Dexter is worried that someone might have witnessed his killing. Rita is trying to deal with her ex-husband.

episodes.name: Circle of Friends
episodes.overview: Rita must deal with the return of her menacing, recently paroled husband, Paul Bennett. The Ice Truck Killer is supposedly identified, but Dexter is skeptical.

episodes.name: Shrink Wrap
episodes.overview: The strange suicide of a powerful woman leads Dexter to suspect her shrink of murder, but he gets a shock when a visit to the suspect opens up dark secrets from Dexter's past.

episodes.name: Father Knows Best
episodes.overview: While on a weekend getaway with Rita, his sister and her new beau, Dexter learns that his biological father, thought to be dead for 30 years, has just passed away, and that he's willed his son everything he owned.

episodes.name: Seeing Red
episodes.overview: Dexter Morgan is sent to investigate a blood-soaked crime scene, and is gripped by a suppressed memory. Meanwhile, Rita is charged with assaulting Paul and could lose the children in a custody battle. Angel investigates a hunch regarding The Ice Truck Killer.

episodes.name: Truth Be Told
episodes.overview: The Ice Truck Killer sets a trap for Dexter by kidnapping someone close to him; Dexter's increasingly odd behavior piques Doakes's interest.

episodes.name: Born Free
episodes.overview: Dexter figures out who the Ice Truck Killer is, along with a dark secret from his troubled past. Meanwhile, La Guerta is replaced with a new boss and Angel figures out a way how to track the killer while he's still in hospital.

episodes.name: It's Alive!
episodes.overview: Things are really beginning to heat up for Dexter: Doakes' suspicions about him are growing, his victims are escaping, and his body dump site has been found. Meanwhile Paul is troubling Rita over the missing shoe and Debra is going through trauma over the Ice Truck killer incident.

episodes.name: Waiting to Exhale
episodes.overview: Dexter is finding it hard to have closure on killing his brother. There is a new FBI agent to head the "Bay Harbor Butcher" case. Debra is finding it tough to put the past behind her.

episodes.name: An Inconvenient Lie
episodes.overview: Dexter's inability to deceive has him pursuing a slick liar as his next victim, while Rita believes he's concealing a drug problem and forces him into a 12-step program where he meets Lila, a seductive new woman.

episodes.name: See-Through
episodes.overview: Rita's estranged mother visits and senses something is wrong with Dexter. Masuka thinks he's developed a lead in the Bay Harbor Butcher case, which has Dexter concerned. Angel's interrogation of a witness crosses a line.

episodes.name: The Dark Defender
episodes.overview: Dexter discovers that the man who murdered his mother in front of him as a boy is still alive and confronts the killer as part of his recovery from addiction - but he also discovers that old impulses die hard.

episodes.name: Dex, Lies, and Videotape
episodes.overview: A copy-cat killer follows the lead of the "Bay Harbor Butcher." Dexter must destroy an incriminating surveillance video.

episodes.name: That Night, a Forest Grew
episodes.overview: Dexter manages to get Doakes off his back. He devises a plan to get the police in another direction. Lila and Dex's relationship intensifies, and he has some interaction with Rita and the kids.

episodes.name: Morning Comes
episodes.overview: Dexter is attacked by the man who murdered his mother. Meanwhile, Lila wants to get closer to Dex, and Debra and Lundy close in on the Bay Harbor Butcher.

episodes.name: Resistance Is Futile
episodes.overview: Dexter realizes it won't be easy to end things completely with Lila. Dexter tries to stay ahead of the investigations as the manhunt for the Bay Harbor Butcher intensifies. An enemy of Dexter figures out his secret.

episodes.name: There's Something About Harry
episodes.overview: Doakes follows Dexter to his next crime scene and shocks him with a revelation about Harry. The Bay Harbor Butcher task force feels they are zeroing in on their man.

episodes.name: Left Turn Ahead
episodes.overview: Dexter must make a massive decision that will have an effect on all those he holds close. Lila meanwhile is back with a secret to blackmail Dexter.

episodes.name: The British Invasion
episodes.overview: Special Agent Lundy and the FBI finally settle the case of the Bay Harbor Butcher, but the heat's not entirely off Dexter as the flames literally close in.

episodes.name: Our Father
episodes.overview: Dexter has put his life back together but things may fall apart soon. His new victim is also being targeted by Assistant District Attorney Miguel Prado. Deb has to decide how to handle Internal Affairs, as they want her to turn on her new partner.

episodes.name: Finding Freebo
episodes.overview: Debra and the homicide unit, Dexter and ADA Miguel Prado are all searching for the same serial killer, who is responsible for the death of Prado's younger brother. Meanwhile at home, Dexter is faced with a decision to make concerning Rita and her kid

episodes.name: The Lion Sleeps Tonight
episodes.overview: Dexter and a pedophile have a run-in; Dexter is unable to restore Freebo's good name after he is accused of a murder he didn't commit. Also, Dexter's friendship with Miguel weighs on his mind.

episodes.name: All in the Family
episodes.overview: After an unsuccessful marriage proposal, Dexter must convince Rita that he is looking for more than a convenient merger of finances and parenthood.

episodes.name: Turning Biminese
episodes.overview: Dexter hears the story of a husband who has gotten away with murdering two wives for financial gain and tracks the man to Bimini, but Rita has a medical emergency while Dexter has disappeared.

episodes.name: Sí Se Puede
episodes.overview: Dexter tests Miguel to see if he is on his dark-side job. Rita questions her career path after being fired from her job. Debra believes she in some way caused another homicide while working on her current case.

episodes.name: Easy as Pie
episodes.overview: Dexter angers Miguel Prado when he says no to his request for them to kill Ellen Wolf. Meanwhile, a deathly ill Camilla Figg asks Dexter to kill her. Anton Briggs is told that he is being used as bait to get at The Skinner.

episodes.name: The Damage a Man Can Do
episodes.overview: Dexter undertakes to teach Miguel Prado the Code, but doesn't realize he's whetting his thirst for blood; Debra is one step closer to finding the Skinner; Quinn reveals a secret about C.I. Anton Briggs.

episodes.name: About Last Night
episodes.overview: Dexter is happy to have someone to help him with his killings, but can his new accomplice really keep to the Code of Harry or will he become the very thing Dexter seeks to destroy? Rita revolves to confront Miguel about his suspected infidelity. Debra searches furiously for Anton when it's revealed that he was abducted by the Skinner.

episodes.name: Go Your Own Way
episodes.overview: A series of chess-like moves ensues when Dexter and Miguel vie for the upper hand, with Miguel winning Rita's affection by presenting her with a lavish wedding gift; Debra wonders if her relationship with Anton is worth the trouble.

episodes.name: I Had a Dream
episodes.overview: Dexter must find a way to get rid of Miguel despite the fact that his friend is a highly placed district attorney and also Dexter's best man, and Debra finds a new clue to the Skinner's identity.

episodes.name: Do You Take Dexter Morgan?
episodes.overview: Dexter attempts to calm the unstable Ramon now that Miguel is out of the picture; Debra is about to receive her detective's shield but is blind-sided with information that could derail her promotion.

episodes.name: Living the Dream
episodes.overview: Dexter sleeplessly struggles to balance his new family life with his dark, murderous drive for a fresh victim, and his busier-than-ever forensic career pursuing a new target, "The Trinity Killer."

episodes.name: Remains to Be Seen
episodes.overview: Dexter realizes that he has carelessly misplaced crucial evidence that could expose his dark side to the world, while helping to investigate new slayings that Agent Frank Lundy insists are the work of Trinity.

episodes.name: Blinded by the Light
episodes.overview: Dexter is hampered by his neighborhood's increased vigilance due to vandalism, his temporary inability to drive himself anywhere, and his admiration for the killing technique of the artful Trinity Killer.

episodes.name: Dex Takes a Holiday
episodes.overview: Rita goes to a wedding with the kids, allowing Dexter to have his own little vacation. He does what he does best when alone, hunt, and goes after an officer with a dark secret.

episodes.name: Dirty Harry
episodes.overview: Dexter is inspired to investigate the Trinity Killer on his own, while Debra blames herself for an event that was out of her control, and Rita realizes how little she knows her husband after discovering one of his secrets.

episodes.name: If I Had a Hammer
episodes.overview: Dexter doubles his efforts to stay ahead of the official Trinity investigation, and receives relationship advice about the friction between himself and Rita from a most unexpected source.

episodes.name: Slack Tide
episodes.overview: Dexter finally strikes a balance between family, career and his secret life, but is thwarted in his pursuit of his next victim, while Debra expresses a renewed and unwelcome interest in their father's checkered past. Miami Metro Homicide tries to solve the Model Killer Case.

episodes.name: Road Kill
episodes.overview: Dexter wonders if his father's Code is truly the right path, and hopes to get answers by accompanying Trinity on an out-of-town road trip to Tampa; Debra discovers that her single-minded pursuit of Trinity has blinded her to the true identity of her assailant.

episodes.name: Hungry Man
episodes.overview: Dexter learns more about Arthur's bizarre and twisted world, while Rita has her hands full preparing Thanksgiving dinner for a full house, Debra uses the holiday to further investigate Trinity, and Batista gets closure on an old case.

episodes.name: Lost Boys
episodes.overview: The disappearance of a ten-year-old boy forces Dexter to question his assumptions about Trinity, while Cody defends one of Dexter's lies and Masuka is unable to face what he witnessed on Thanksgiving.

episodes.name: Hello, Dexter Morgan
episodes.overview: Dexter must buy time to protect himself when Debra's investigation brings the department one step away from identifying Arthur as the Trinity Killer; Rita confides in Dexter, but is disappointed in the result.

episodes.name: The Getaway
episodes.overview: Dexter and Arthur are on a collision course.

episodes.name: My Bad
episodes.overview: Dexter must make a choice; Quinn stirs up trouble for Dexter but supports Deb.

episodes.name: Hello, Bandit
episodes.overview: Dexter tries to focus on the children and fight his dark urges; Debra ends up on Quinn's doorstep.

episodes.name: Practically Perfect
episodes.overview: Dexter hires a nanny; Debra is annoyed with her new partner; Quinn continues to investigate Rita's murder.

episodes.name: Beauty and the Beast
episodes.overview: Dexter must save a life; Deb has a confrontation with a key suspect; Quinn follows up on the similarities between Dexter and Kyle Butler.

episodes.name: First Blood
episodes.overview: Dexter is saddled with an unwanted conspirator; Dexter worries about how Rita's death will affect Harrison; Deb works alone; Quinn enlists an old friend's help.

episodes.name: Everything Is Illumenated
episodes.overview: Dexter is drawn into a precarious situation; Batista discovers an interesting lead; Quinn gets information about Dexter from a questionable source.

episodes.name: Circle Us
episodes.overview: Dexter is called to investigate a horrifying crime scene; the Santa Muerte case leads to a violent standoff between Debra and the Santa Muerte killers.

episodes.name: Take It!
episodes.overview: Dexter and Lumen vet and stalk a violent killer; Debra gets into some unexpected trouble from the fallout of the Santa Muerte case

episodes.name: Teenage Wasteland
episodes.overview: Dexter and Lumen hunt for their next victim; Debra uncovers new evidence in the Barrel Girl case

episodes.name: In the Beginning
episodes.overview: Homicide uncovers some key evidence linked to one of Dexter's and Lumen's prior victims; Debra identifies two more suspects in the case.

episodes.name: Hop a Freighter
episodes.overview: Dexter must do damage control; Debra's speculation begins to take shape; Quinn becomes involved in a homicide.

episodes.name: The Big One
episodes.overview: Dexter realizes that he and Lumen are being lured into a trap and risks everything to escape; Quinn needs help that only Dexter can provide.

episodes.name: Those Kinds of Things
episodes.overview: Dexter attends his high school reunion to hunt a former Prom King; an investigation into a faith-based murder causes Dexter to ask some spiritual questions; Debra becomes an unexpected hero.

episodes.name: Once Upon a Time...
episodes.overview: Brother Sam, a minister with a criminal history, is brought in by the homicide department to help solve a macabre murder, but Dexter sees him for what he really is, and it's definitely not a man of God. Elsewhere, Debra's sudden hero status elicits a pair of surprising proposals.

episodes.name: Smokey and the Bandit
episodes.overview: Dexter is confronted with a sobering glimpse of his own potential future when a serial killer from his past makes a startling reappearance; Debra is uncomfortable in her new job; Travis struggles to keep his mentor happy, as he and Gellar prepare a new twisted tableau, ensuring Debra's next task will be a daunting one.

episodes.name: A Horse of a Different Color
episodes.overview: Much to his own surprise, an emergency with Harrison and a new tableau from Gellar and Travis has Dexter leaning on Brother Sam and an unexpected winged messenger for support as he questions the idea of faith; with proof of a religiously motivated killer, Homicide hunts for a zealot, with Debra giving her first official press conference.

episodes.name: The Angel of Death
episodes.overview: With the help of his newfound friend Brother Sam, Dexter wonders if there is light within him to counter the darkness, while the search for the Doomsday Killers takes him in a new direction; Batista and Quinn pay a visit to the university where Professor Gellar taught; due to departmental regulations following the shooting, Debra is forced to begin therapy.

episodes.name: Just Let Go
episodes.overview: Dexter is caught up in a very personal case that awakens the needs of his Dark Passenger; Debra feels overwhelmed by her new Lieutenant duties, made all the more complicated when she finds out Quinn slept with a witness in the Doomsday case.

episodes.name: Nebraska
episodes.overview: Dexter finds himself teamed up with a fellow Dark Passenger when he takes a road trip to Nebraska to tie up some loose ends from the past; Debra deals with the complications of her promotion as she leads the Doomsday investigation.

episodes.name: Sin of Omission
episodes.overview: Dexter uses lessons he learned from Brother Sam to follow up on some new leads in the Doomsday investigation; Debra butts heads with Captain LaGuerta over the case of a dead call girl.

episodes.name: Get Gellar
episodes.overview: Dexter receives help from an unexpected source while hunting the Doomsday Killers and staying one step ahead of Homicide; Debra discovers something new about herself in therapy.

episodes.name: Ricochet Rabbit
episodes.overview: Dexter tries to figure out the Doomsday Killers' next victim before it's too late; Debra has a strong reaction to a crime scene, which leads her to the realization that she leans too heavily on her brother.

episodes.name: Talk to the Hand
episodes.overview: Dexter finds that in order to catch the Doomsday Killers, he must create a macabre tableau of his own; Debra's battle with LaGuerta over the case of the dead call girl boils over, and her therapist makes an unnerving suggestion.

episodes.name: This Is the Way the World Ends
episodes.overview: Dexter and Homicide race against a lunar eclipse to catch the Doomsday Killers before their final gruesome act; Debra struggles with a new emotional reality.

episodes.name: Are You...?
episodes.overview: After witnessing her brother kill, Debra attempts to reconcile with Dexter while struggling to cover up their involvement with the murder.

episodes.name: Sunshine and Frosty Swirl
episodes.overview: Miami Metro sets out to unearth new evidence on a claim from a local convict that he has new information regarding a 15-year-old crime spree; Debra tries to cure Dexter of his killer tendencies.

episodes.name: Buck the System
episodes.overview: Dexter tries to bring Debra on board with his new target. While the Ukrainian mob seeks revenge for the killing of one of their own, Quinn grows close to a dancer at their strip club.

episodes.name: Run
episodes.overview: After capturing a deadly killer, things go awry for Miami Metro, sending Debra into a tailspin. In an effort to help his sister, Dexter enters into a dangerous game of cat and mouse, while the Ukrainian mob continues to seek revenge.

episodes.name: Swim Deep
episodes.overview: While trying to uncover why someone was killed on his boat, Dexter must out-maneuver a vengeful Isaak. New leads are brought to light on the Wayne Randall case by Hannah McKay, Randall's alluring former accomplice who Dexter discovers has a secret.

episodes.name: Do the Wrong Thing
episodes.overview: Debra gets to know a local crime writer who's dug up some incriminating dirt on Hannah McKay (Dexter's newest obsession). Quinn gets an offer he tries to refuse and Batista is drawn to a new business opportunity. LaGuerta keeps digging into the Bay Harbor Butcher case.

episodes.name: Chemistry
episodes.overview: Quinn falls back into his old ways as he makes some questionable choices to protect Nadia. Things heat up as Dexter and Hannah grow closer, but when Sal Price discovers the two are involved, he wants the exclusive story.

episodes.name: Argentina
episodes.overview: Fresh out of jail, Isaak renews his pursuit of Dexter while Quinn continues his power struggle with the Koshkas. Dexter tries to keep Debra in the dark about his relationship with Hannah, but a surprise visit from Astor, Cody and Harrison throws a wrench into his plans.

episodes.name: Helter Skelter
episodes.overview: As Dexter scrambles to track down Isaak, a power struggle erupts among the Koshka Brotherhood, which could prove to Dexter's advantage. Meanwhile, Miami Dade tries to smoke out the Phantom Arsonist.

episodes.name: The Dark... Whatever
episodes.overview: Dexter is unnerved when Hannah's father pays him a surprise visit; the Phantom Arsonist's crimes become more vicious; and Quinn defends Nadia's honor and finds himself in a volatile situation. Meanwhile; LaGuerta asks a former superior for help on the Bay Harbor Butcher case.

episodes.name: Do You See What I See?
episodes.overview: As Christmas approaches, Dexter tries to balance his life with Hannah and his relationship with Debra. Dexter learns the man responsible for ordering his mother's death has been released from jail.

episodes.name: Surprise, Motherfucker!
episodes.overview: Dexter must protect himself when LaGuerta closes in on his secret.

episodes.name: A Beautiful Day
episodes.overview: It’s been 6 months since LaGuerta’s murder – and Dexter is still managing life as a dad, brother, and serial killer. Debra now works as a PI for a private firm and Batista has replaced her as Lieutenant. Meanwhile, Miami Metro investigates the murder of a man who has had pieces of his brain removed.

episodes.name: Every Silver Lining...
episodes.overview: Miami Metro continues their hunt for the Brain Surgeon, while Dr. Vogel enlists Dexter to do her bidding based on her own personal experience with her previous patients. Debra continues to track down her PI case and has to take matters into her own hands.

episodes.name: What's Eating Dexter Morgan?
episodes.overview: Dexter continues his manhunt for the Brain Surgeon. Dr. Vogel tries to prove to Dexter that he’s perfect as a psychopath. Debra’s PTSD kicks in and she makes a desperate plea to confess to LaGuerta’s murder.

episodes.name: Scar Tissue
episodes.overview: Dexter tracks down another potential serial killer from Dr. Vogel’s list. Quinn celebrates his passing of the Sergeant’s exam by defending Debra’s honor in a fight. Dr. Vogel begins treating Debra for her PTSD.

episodes.name: This Little Piggy
episodes.overview: Dr. Vogel gets abducted by the Brain Surgeon, while Dexter and Debra try and rescue her. Masuka finds out he has a daughter.

episodes.name: A Little Reflection
episodes.overview: Dexter monitors a young psychopath to see if he is suited for his table. Dexter and Debra finally get back to normal, but are surprised by his ex, Hannah.

episodes.name: Dress Code
episodes.overview: Dexter hunts down Hannah to figure out why she’s back in Miami. Dexter also takes on a protégé and starts teaching him the Code.

episodes.name: Are We There Yet?
episodes.overview: Dexter tracks down his protégé in the belief that he murdered his neighbor. Meanwhile, Dexter tries to help Hannah escape the country.

episodes.name: Make Your Own Kind of Music
episodes.overview: Dexter investigates a murder that hits close to home. Meanwhile, Dr. Vogel gets the surprise of her life when someone from her past returns.

episodes.name: Goodbye Miami
episodes.overview: Dexter enlists Dr. Vogel's help to lure the Brain Surgeon to a location in order to get him on the table. Later, an important person in Dexter's life is murdered before his eyes.

episodes.name: Monkey in a Box
episodes.overview: Dexter is torn between fleeing the country with Hannah and Harrison, and taking Saxon out once and for all.

episodes.name: Remember the Monsters?
episodes.overview: Dexter is faced with impossible odds as a brewing hurricane makes its way towards Miami.

episodes.name: Cold Snap
episodes.overview: For the past decade, Dexter has been living a quiet, isolated life far away from the temptations of his past. When a local hotshot begins behaving recklessly, and a mysterious stranger seems to be on Dexter's trail, he questions whether he can continue to suppress the murderous urgings of his Dark Passenger.

episodes.name: Storm of Fuck
episodes.overview: When a simple missing person case turns into a crime scene, Dexter's cabin becomes home base for the search and rescue. Terrible timing for Dexter, who is trying to reunite with his long lost son, Harrison. As Angela and Harrison become acquainted, Dexter struggles to keep his old and new worlds separate.

episodes.name: Smoke Signals
episodes.overview: Dexter hopes to have a chance to make things right with Harrison, which is proving to be tough to do when there's a full-fledged crime investigation that Dexter himself has caused. Harrison joins the high school wrestling team and quickly makes a name for himself at school. Meanwhile, a true crime podcaster from LA arrives in Iron Lake and befriends Dexter's police chief girlfriend.

episodes.name: H Is for Hero
episodes.overview: The father of the person whom Dexter killed makes a surprising announcement which could prove to be a huge problem for Dexter. Meanwhile, Dexter discovers Harrison was involved in a serious incident at the high school. And now this young newcomer is considered a hero to the entire town. Angela digs deeper into a missing persons case.

episodes.name: Runaway
episodes.overview: Dexter's method of protecting his son from drugs unleashes his Dark Passenger in a very impulsive way. Meanwhile Angela and Molly take a trip to New York City that leaves them wondering about a well-respected member of the community.

episodes.name: Too Many Tuna Sandwiches
episodes.overview: Someone in Iron Lake has discovered Jim Lindsay's secret identity, sending Dexter on the prowl to find out who it was. This pursuit leads him to realize that he might not be the only serial killer in town. Meanwhile Harrison spirals out of control during a wrestling match and Angela makes a dark discovery of her own.

episodes.name: Skin of Her Teeth
episodes.overview: Dexter is worried that a serial killer may have set its sights on someone he cares deeply about. So, he turns from predator to protector... a role he's not comfortable playing. Meanwhile Angela arrests someone from Iron Lake who may be the culprit of a cold case that is near and dear to her heart.  Dexter and Harrison run into some more bumps in the road in their relationship, leading Harrison to turn to a very dangerous person as his father figure.

episodes.name: Unfair Game
episodes.overview: Dexter fights for his life in the woods of Iron Lake, leading to a confrontation in an abandoned summer camp. Is there a better place for a serial killer to confront another violent killer? Harrison finds himself at a crossroads between two father figures who can lead him down two very different paths. Meanwhile Angela starts to make some disturbing discoveries of her own.

episodes.name: The Family Business
episodes.overview: Dexter and Harrison find themselves closer than ever over Christmas break. What kind of gifts will this father and son give to one another? But this brings this father and son into the crosshairs of a very violent and scary serial killer. Angela starts to wonder if Iron Lake is not the cozy and safe place she always thought it was.

episodes.name: Sins of the Father
episodes.overview: Dexter and Harrison try to live a normal life in a place that they have discovered is not as normal as they thought it was. Will the two of them be able to live happily ever after, despite all the threats coming their way?

episodes.name: And in the beginning...
episodes.overview: Young Dexter Morgan struggles to keep his urges in check while navigating life with his father, Harry, and sister, high-school senior Deb. After Harry has a health scare, Dexter realizes it may finally be time to fulfill his destiny.

episodes.name: Kid in a Candy Store
episodes.overview: Dexter adjusts to his new job as a forensics intern at Miami Metro, as Harry returns to work after his medical leave. Deb lashes out at her dad by engaging in petty criminal behavior.

episodes.name: Miami Vice
episodes.overview: Dexter goes after a loan shark with ties to Batista, as Harry is told to keep an eye on Maria Laguerta, Miami Metro's newest homicide detective. Deb throws a party to win the favor of her volleyball teammates.

episodes.name: Fender Bender
episodes.overview: Dexter targets a retired mob hitman (who may still be killing), as Harry works the gruesome murder case of a 10-year-old boy. Deb sneaks into a nightclub with Sofia and meets an exciting new friend.

episodes.name: F Is for Fuck-Up
episodes.overview: After a kill goes wrong, Dexter is grounded by Harry. Deb deepens her relationship with Gio, and bonds with her brother on the anniversary of their mother's death.

episodes.name: The Joy of Killing
episodes.overview: Miami Metro is shaken by a kidnapping that hits close to home, as Dexter sets his sights on a killer that slipped through the cracks of the justice system. But first, Dexter must survive a double date with Deb and her new boyfriend.

episodes.name: The Big Bad Body Problem
episodes.overview: Dexter needs to find a new hiding place after his most recent dumping ground is blown. Harry and LaGuerta pitch a controversial theory to Spencer, as the hunt for a missing child continues. Deb gets in trouble for an outburst in volleyball practice.

episodes.name: Business and Pleasure
episodes.overview: Deb sneaks away for a trip with her mysterious boyfriend. Dexter is tasked with finding her. Harry and LaGuerta travel to Tampa to look into a potential serial killer on the loose, while Miami Metro closes in on suspects in the child kidnapping.

episodes.name: Blood Drive
episodes.overview: In the fallout of a SWAT raid gone wrong, Dexter closes in on the child kidnapper. Meanwhile, Deb goes to work with her Dad... and sees Miami Metro in a new light. Harry digs deeper into the serial killer case but keeps his theory from LaGuerta.

episodes.name: Code Blues
episodes.overview: Dexter races to find the missing kidnapped child before it's too late. Deb visits her godfather in the hospital and rethinks her future. Harry comes face-to-face with a serial killer... leading to a shocking result.

episodes.name: A Beating Heart...
episodes.overview: Dexter Morgan awakens from a ten-week coma.

episodes.name: Camera Shy
episodes.overview: Dexter tracks down a serial killer in NYC as Harrison spirals from guilt.

episodes.name: Backseat Driver
episodes.overview: Dexter must carefully balance attempts to help get him acclimated.

episodes.name: Call Me Red
episodes.overview: Dexter attends a mysterious gathering as Harrison struggles at work.

episodes.name: Murder Horny
episodes.overview: Harrison's fears escalate as Dexter pursues a dangerous connection.

episodes.name: Cats & Mouse
episodes.overview: As Dexter closes in on a murderer, Harrison faces a crisis of identity.

episodes.name: Course Correction
episodes.overview: Dexter learns unsettling information as Harrison contemplates his future.

episodes.name: The Kill Room Where It Happens
episodes.overview: Dexter redirects his attention as a way to step up as a father.

episodes.name: Touched by an Ángel
episodes.overview: Prater and Charley's revelation forces Dexter to go to extreme lengths.

episodes.name: And Justice for All...
episodes.overview: Dexter and Harrison face the consequences of their darkness.

episodes.name: How to Start a Dragon Academy
episodes.overview: Dragons are still dragons, and as a result, they are causing mayhem in the Isle of Berk. Stoick is reconsidering allowing the dragons to roam freely on Berk. It is up to Hiccup and his friends to convince Stoick otherwise.

episodes.name: Viking for Hire
episodes.overview: The peace with the dragons has put Gobber out of business, since his job was making weapons to fight dragons. Stoick asks Hiccup to help find a new job for him ... but unfortunately a change of profession does not seem to go well with Gobber.

episodes.name: Animal House
episodes.overview: The dragons unintentionally reduce Berk's food productivity by still scaring the living daylights out of the local livestock, and with the first winter storm coming, the dragon-riding Viking youths of Berk must try and familiarize the local animals with their Dragon companions.

episodes.name: The Terrible Twos
episodes.overview: Hiccup finds an injured baby dragon. Toothless does not warm up to the energetic newcomer and the baby's angry mother arrives looking for her lost son.

episodes.name: In Dragons We Trust
episodes.overview: The Dragons are blamed for vandalism, and Hiccup tries to prove their innocence by forming a night patrol called the "Dragon United Monitoring Brigade" (DUMB), but not in time to prevent the dragons' banishment from the island. Hiccup begins to suspect that Mildew is responsible for the scapegoat.

episodes.name: Alvin and the Outcasts
episodes.overview: Hiccup had discovered Mildew framed the dragons but has destroyed all evidence of it, therefore he must prove of their innocence in order to get them back. Meanwhile, Alvin the Treacherous of the Outcast Tribe are on the mission to raid the Isle of Berk in order to find and capture the "Dragon Conqueror".

episodes.name: How to Pick Your Dragon
episodes.overview: Stoick is in need of learning how to ride a dragon, and he quickly insists on riding Toothless all the time. Meanwhile, a mysterious dragon has been attacking fishing ships.

episodes.name: Portrait of Hiccup as a Buff Man
episodes.overview: After getting his picture painted with his father, Hiccup begins to fear that he is not Stoick's ideal son, so to prove himself, he and his friends set out on a treasure hunt that even Stoick had not been able to complete.

episodes.name: Dragon Flower
episodes.overview: After Mildew poisons all the dragons on the island, he is forced to help Hiccup, Stoick, and Gobber find and capture the ferocious Scauldron in order to get the antidote.

episodes.name: Heather Report (1)
episodes.overview: The teens of Berk find a mysterious girl named Heather on Thor's Beach. While Hiccup and the other teens openly welcome the newcomer, Astrid's jealousy turns to suspicion when she notices Heather is taking an unusual interest in the Book of Dragons.

episodes.name: Heather Report (2)
episodes.overview: Heather's betrayal to Berk caused the Book of Dragons to fall into Alvin the Treacherous' hands, and Heather's imprisonment on Berk. In order to get the book back from Alvin, Astrid disguises herself as Heather and goes into Outcast Territory. Towards the end of her mission, Astrid learns and confirms Heather's true motives for helping the Outcasts.

episodes.name: Thawfest
episodes.overview: Berk's annual festive sporting competition, the Thawfest games, is underway and all the teens are participating with the dragons for the first time in Viking history. Hiccup is determined to finally best Snotlout, who constantly gloats about being the reigning Thawfest champion, but Hiccup's successes begin to make him arrogant and boastful, much to Astrid's displeasure and disappointment.

episodes.name: When Lightning Strikes
episodes.overview: Sudden lightning storms are endangering the residents of Berk and it is believed Thor is angry at the island. Mildew leads the superstitious villagers into blaming Toothless, "the unholy offspring of lightning and death itself", for the lightning, leaving Hiccup to find the true answers for Thor's sudden destructive anger.

episodes.name: What Flies Beneath
episodes.overview: A rogue dragon – a Whispering Death – shows up in Berk, tearing up the village. He's on a mission to find Toothless and settle a score. Despite Hiccup's efforts to help, Toothless insists on facing the dragon alone. But unlike their past battle, Toothless's inability to fly my prove to be his undoing.

episodes.name: Twinsanity
episodes.overview: Dagur the Deranged visits Berk to renew a peace treaty between the Berserkers and Berk, and the dragons are put into hiding to dispel rumours that Berk is training a dragon army. But when Ruffnut and Tuffnut begin to fight, their dragon, Barf and Belch, is left out of control. The Viking youths must bring the twins back together to control their dragon and avoid provoking Dagur to war.

episodes.name: Defiant One
episodes.overview: Caught in a waterspout, Toothless' prosthetic tail is damaged, rendering him unable to fly and thus leaving him, Hiccup and Snotlout stranded on Outcast Island. The three must work together to repair the damage, overcome the rivalry between Hiccup and Snotlout and return home to Berk before they are captured by Alvin and the Outcasts.

episodes.name: Breakneck Bog
episodes.overview: When Hiccup discovers Trader Johann's ship was shipwrecked in a rumored haunted bog with a gift made for him by his mother, he will stop at nothing to retrieve it.

episodes.name: Gem of a Different Color
episodes.overview: When Snotlout and the villagers mistake dragon eggs for gems that bring good fortune, Fishlegs, who is responsible for the eggs' discovery, must take a stand against Snotlout and reunite the eggs with their Changewing mothers before they destroy Berk.

episodes.name: We Are Family (1)
episodes.overview: During Berk's annual Bork week celebrations, Hiccup is granted access to Bork the Bold's private and personal archives. When he finds information and a map about an island inhabited by Night Furies within Bork's notes, Hiccup is determined to bring Toothless to his family.

episodes.name: We Are Family (2)
episodes.overview: Mildew has betrayed the Vikings of Berk and allowed Alvin the Treacherous to kidnap Hiccup and Toothless. Astrid and Stoick must rescue them from Outcast Island along with help from a new friend.

episodes.name: Live and Let Fly
episodes.overview: Certain that Alvin is planning an attack on Berk, Stoick institutes flight restrictions on the dragon academy. Hiccup, certain that Alvin will be using his newfound dragon-training knowledge to bolster his attack, forms a secret “Dragon Flight Club” in order to train the kids to defend Berk against Alvin’s dragon army.

episodes.name: The Iron Gronckle
episodes.overview: When Meatlug accidentally ingests magnetic rocks, she becomes magnetic herself - causing destruction through the town, but ultimately helping the kids win a battle against the Outcasts.

episodes.name: The Night and the Fury
episodes.overview: The kids go on a dragon survival-training mission to Dragon Island, where they encounter Dagur, the Berserker Chief, who has become obsessed with hunting the Night Fury.

episodes.name: Tunnel Vision
episodes.overview: Hiccup discovers that the town's water shortage has been caused by the Outcasts' plan to hide Whispering Death eggs under Berk, which soon hatch and tunnel under the island, threatening the villagers.

episodes.name: Race to Fireworm Island
episodes.overview: Hookfang is losing his ability to catch on fire, and it's up to Snotlout, Hiccup and the others to find the lair of the Fireworm Queen, which may hold the only possible cure, before it's too late.

episodes.name: Fright of Passage
episodes.overview: Hiccup must help Astrid face her fear of the terrifying Flightmare, a ghostly dragon that seemingly "froze" her uncle with fear, shaming her family name. With Fishlegs' help, they discover that there's more to the Flightmare than meets the eye

episodes.name: Worst in Show
episodes.overview: A Terrible Terror training competition leads to a heated rivalry between Fishlegs and Snotlout, allowing Alvin to kidnap Meatlug. The kids must use their newly trained Terrors to rescue their friend.

episodes.name: Appetite for Destruction
episodes.overview: When wild dragons start overwhelming Berk, Hiccup and Toothless discover islands are disappearing throughout the archipelago. When Hiccup and Toothless investigate the cause, they then encounter the Screaming Death.

episodes.name: Zippleback Down
episodes.overview: While searching for traps, Tuffnut, Barf and Belch get trapped themselves... and a forest fire is approaching.

episodes.name: A View to a Skrill (1)
episodes.overview: When the dragon riders discover a frozen Skrill, a lightning-powered dragon revered by the Berserkers, they scramble to get it off Berk.

episodes.name: A View to a Skrill (2)
episodes.overview: Tuffnut goes undercover to help the riders rescue a Skrill.

episodes.name: The Flight Stuff
episodes.overview: Snotlout becomes convinced he is dying, and leaves Hookfang to Gustav. But when he "miraculously" recovers, Snotlout takes his dragon back, and Gustav decides to get a dragon of his own and join the academy... whether they want him or not.

episodes.name: Free Scauldy
episodes.overview: Hiccup and co. discover an injured Scauldron on the shores of Changewing Island. The kids must heal the dragon and return him safely to the ocean, before he falls victim to the elements, as well as the territorial Changewings.

episodes.name: Frozen
episodes.overview: Hiccup and Toothless return from a mission to find Berk mysteriously empty. They discover that the village has been overrun by Speed Stingers, a venomous non-flying dragon that has trekked across the frozen sea to Berk.

episodes.name: A Tale of Two Dragons
episodes.overview: Hookfang and Stormfly quarrel while the town prepares for the harvest.

episodes.name: The Eel Effect
episodes.overview: Berk has been plagued with a bad case of the Eel Pox. Hiccup, Toothless and the gang, along with Gothi, go gather the ingredients for the cure. There's just one problem; the final ingredient is an eel from Eel Island.

episodes.name: Smoke Gets in Your Eyes
episodes.overview: The mysterious theft of metal objects all over Berk reveals the presence of marauding Smokebreaths. Hiccup and Toothless soon discover that these dragons are the work of Dagur, in a plot to render Berk defenseless.

episodes.name: Bing! Bam! Boom!
episodes.overview: When three adolescent Thunderdrums attack the village, only Stoick's Thornado can bring chaos to order.

episodes.name: Cast Out (1)
episodes.overview: Snotlout is expelled from the academy; Alvin returns to Berk to make amends.

episodes.name: Cast Out (2)
episodes.overview: Alvin reveals he trained the Screaming Death to attack Berk and destroy the island's inhabitants and only Snotlout and Hookfang can save Berk before it's too late. Meanwhile Hiccup gets ready for a final fight against Dagur to save Toothless and end the barbarians invasion of Berk once and for all.

episodes.name: Pilot
episodes.overview: Detective Sherlock Holmes, along with his sober companion, Dr. Joan Watson, uses his uncanny ability to read people and analyze crimes to assist the NYPD on some of their more difficult cases. In the season premiere, Holmes consults on a home invasion that resulted in murder.

episodes.name: While You Were Sleeping
episodes.overview: Sherlock uses his powers of deduction to consult on the murder of a young man who is shot upon entering his apartment. Meanwhile, Watson has dinner with her ex-boyfriend, Ty Morstan.

episodes.name: Child Predator
episodes.overview: Sherlock and Watson consult on a child abduction case involving a serial killer who is nicknamed "The Balloon Man," because he leaves a signature batch of balloons at each of his crime scenes.

episodes.name: The Rat Race
episodes.overview: When Sherlock investigates what appears to be a series of murders at a top Wall Street firm, he may himself fall foul of a sociopathic slayer. Joan is set up on a blind date and shows investigative instincts.

episodes.name: Lesser Evils
episodes.overview: Sherlock tries to determine who has been killing weak and terminal patients at a hospital, making it look like they died of natural causes. Meanwhile, Sherlock learns more about Joan’s decision to walk away from her surgical career.

episodes.name: Flight Risk
episodes.overview: Joan is invited to dinner with Sherlock's father. Meanwhile, Sherlock investigates the crash of a small jet, unraveling a mystery beyond a simple accident.

episodes.name: One Way to Get Off
episodes.overview: Sherlock assists with a double homicide that has the same M.O. as a series of murders that took place 13 years ago. Meanwhile, after being frozen out by Sherlock, Watson visits his old rehab center in order to learn more about the mysterious man in her care.

episodes.name: The Long Fuse
episodes.overview: While investigating an explosion that kills two people, Sherlock discovers a crime that’s been lying dormant for years. Meanwhile, Watson encourages Sherlock to finally pick a sponsor.

episodes.name: You Do It to Yourself
episodes.overview: Sherlock uses his trademark deductive reasoning skills to find the killer of a college professor. Meanwhile, Watson gets a call from a former lover who needs her help in disproving hit-and-run charges.

episodes.name: The Leviathan
episodes.overview: Sherlock is called in to investigate when a supposedly uncrackable bank vault called “The Leviathan” is breached. Meanwhile, Joan is pleasantly surprised when Sherlock meets her family and defends her choice to become a sober companion.

episodes.name: Dirty Laundry
episodes.overview: Sherlock and Joan investigate the murder of the general manager of a high-end Manhattan hotel whose body is found inside an industrial laundry machine. Meanwhile, since Joan’s time with Sherlock is almost up, he offers her an apprenticeship.

episodes.name: M.
episodes.overview: Sherlock is reunited with “M.” a British criminal who appears to have followed Sherlock to New York. Meanwhile, Joan has reservations about leaving Sherlock and taking on a new client.

episodes.name: The Red Team
episodes.overview: While on suspension from the NYPD, Sherlock investigates a suspicious hit-and-run accident involving a conspiracy theorist. Meanwhile, Watson tries to broker peace between Sherlock and Gregson.

episodes.name: The Deductionist
episodes.overview: Sherlock pursues Martin Ennis, an unpredictable criminal, before he strikes again. During the investigation, Sherlock is forced to work alongside Kathryn Drummond, the FBI profiler who literally wrote the book on Ennis. Meanwhile, Watson faces eviction from her apartment.

episodes.name: A Giant Gun, Filled with Drugs
episodes.overview: When Sherlock's ex-drug dealer, Rhys, comes around for help, Watson is concerned about Sherlock's sobriety after Rhys implies that Sherlock on drugs made a better detective.

episodes.name: Details
episodes.overview: Sherlock investigates what he believes will be an open-and-shut case after Detective Bell is attacked. However, the case takes an unexpected turn when the suspected assailant is found dead. Meanwhile, Sherlock encourages Joan to learn self-defense.

episodes.name: Possibility Two
episodes.overview: Sherlock investigates a case involving a wealthy philanthropist who believes his incurable illness was given to him on purpose. Meanwhile, Sherlock tries to teach Joan his deductive skills by sending her to a suspicious dry cleaning establishment.

episodes.name: Déjà Vu All Over Again
episodes.overview: Joan investigates her first solo case when a wife disappears. The missing woman left behind a tear-filled video that mentions a murder on a subway platform. While Joan searches for the vanished lady, Sherlock probes the murder.

episodes.name: Snow Angels
episodes.overview: Sherlock and Joan try to prevent the largest cash vault in the U.S. from being robbed during a deadly blizzard. Meanwhile, Holmes gives shelter to Ms. Hudson, a transgender woman who is recovering from her latest breakup.

episodes.name: Dead Man's Switch
episodes.overview: Sherlock and Joan hunt for the accomplice of a murdered blackmailer who targeted families of rape victims. Meanwhile, Sherlock resists celebrating his first year of sobriety.

episodes.name: A Landmark Story
episodes.overview: Sherlock resumes his hunt for Moriarty when Sebastian Moran reveals that a man who was thought to be dead from a heart attack was actually on the list of intended victims from Moriarty.

episodes.name: Risk Management
episodes.overview: Sherlock agrees to take a case from a man claiming to be Moriarty, hoping it will bring him closer to finding his nemesis. Meanwhile, Gregson encourages Joan to give up the dangerous line of work she has chosen and go back to being a sober companion.

episodes.name: The Woman (1)
episodes.overview: As Sherlock reels at the reappearance of his former lover, Irene Adler, a series of flashbacks unravel the tumultuous events that led to his downfall into addiction. Meanwhile, he and Joan once again find themselves in the crosshairs of the enigmatic Moriarty.

episodes.name: Heroine (2)
episodes.overview: As Sherlock reels at the reappearance of his former lover, Irene Adler, a series of flashbacks unravel the tumultuous events that led to his downfall into addiction. Meanwhile, he and Joan once again find themselves in the crosshairs of the enigmatic Moriarty.

episodes.name: Step Nine
episodes.overview: Sherlock is called to London to revisit an old case, where he is forced to face his older brother, Mycroft. Although the siblings suffered a drastic falling-out a few years earlier, Mycroft allows Sherlock and Joan to stay in his new home, 221B Baker Street. With Joan in the middle, the brothers are forced to confront their very complicated history.

episodes.name: Solve for X
episodes.overview: When a well-respected mathematician is found dead, Holmes and Watson set out to solve a murder and the purpose of the highly guarded equation he was trying to decipher. Meanwhile, Watson is conflicted when the son of a former patient approaches her for a loan.

episodes.name: We Are Everyone
episodes.overview: When Holmes and Watson set out to track down a civil contractor who leaked classified information, their lives are disrupted when they are targeted by the group of cyber-activists protecting him. Meanwhile, Joan takes the advice of a friend and signs up for an online dating service.

episodes.name: Poison Pen
